question_text: Which statement best describes what keeps a satellite orbiting Earth?

options.label: A
options.text: The satellite is pushed by engines that fire continuously to keep it moving

options.label: B
options.text: Earth's gravity pulls the satellite towards it, but the satellite's forward motion makes it keep missing Earth

options.label: C
options.text: The satellite is held up by the atmosphere pushing against it

options.label: D
options.text: The satellite orbits because it is weightless and floats around Earth

explanation: A satellite orbits because gravity constantly pulls it downward (towards Earth), but the satellite is also moving sideways very quickly. This combination means the satellite is always falling towards Earth, but it keeps missing it and curves around instead—creating a stable orbit.
